title: Execute pi worker runtime refactor plan task-by-task
notes: Completed Tasks 1-6 of docs/plans/2026-04-16-pi-worker-runtime-refactor-plan.md plus follow-up hardening for Telegram final-answer relay. Then simplified current branch to acpx-only execution path: removed smolvm/backend-registry/tmux execution code, introduced acpx-only worker-runner, routed gateway /run through queued acpx execution, added repeatable acpx smoke test, and verified OrbStack end-to-end for both direct job runs and gateway-queued jobs. Follow-up hardening added serialized acpx runner execution, duplicate-running-job guard, lease heartbeats, runner logging, persistent-mode smoke default, stale lock cleanup, acpx queue default, and current OrbStack pi-worker-instance defaults. Latest takeover synced to OrbStack and passed `pi-worker-instance smoke-acpx takeover-review`. Placeholder API keys in ~/.env.pi were also identified as root cause of broken ACPX auth on guest and documented/fixed in template. Remaining major work still includes deciding how much tmux-based supervisor process management to keep and broader Task 7+ supervisor evolution. Latest docs/naming pass added CONTEXT/glossary/ADRs, rewrote architecture docs, marked ACPX plan statuses, renamed WorkerEnv/WorkerState/workerName, and moved ACPX adapter to acpx/runtime-adapter.

title: Persist ACPX structured job event logs
notes: Added scripts/vm/lib/acpx-event-log.ts and wired ACPX runtime adapter to persist session_ready, normalized AcpRuntimeEvent values, and turn_result records under ~/.pi-worker/jobs/events/<jobId>.ndjson. Verified locally with unit tests and on OrbStack by inspecting event files after smoke.

title: Stream ACPX structured events to Telegram
notes: Use AcpRuntimeEvent stream plus existing telegram-formatter helpers to emit/throttle thought snippets, tool status, usage/status, and final answer chunks instead of only sending final answer.

title: Replace global acpx runner lock with per-session turn locking
notes: Worker runner now derives turn lock key from agent+chatId for persistent sessions and jobId for oneshot, allowing independent sessions to run concurrently while preserving same-session ordering. Added worker-runner test for concurrent same-chat jobs.

title: Add acpx session reset and runtime control commands
notes: Implemented /reset for gateway and Telegram using runtime.close({ discardPersistentState: true }) via resetWorkerChatSession. Remaining: consider /mode and /set model/config using runtime.setMode and setConfigOption.

title: Use AbortController for acpx job timeout and cancellation
notes: runAttempt now passes AbortSignal to runtime.startTurn, aborts on timeout, and combines worker cancel with controller.abort() plus turn.cancel(). Verified typecheck/tests and OrbStack smoke.

title: Evaluate acpx attachments and flow-run support
notes: Defer until core runtime is stable. Attachments enable Telegram image/file use cases. FlowRunner is real and persists run bundles, but should be reserved for explicit multi-step workflow features.

title: Expose pi-worker VM as ACP-compatible dogfood agent
notes: Added ACP stdio adapter, gateway event polling/cancel endpoints, text-only event mapper, smoke test, and pi-worker-acp wrapper. VM dogfood review found prompt shell-injection, silent failed-job UX, and queued-cancel race; fixed with temp-file prompt transport, explicit failed-job text/status handling, cancel-before-start guard, and regression test. Event polling scalability remains follow-up.
